name: MPS — Memory Pool System as Pluggable GC Framework
summary: Ravenbrook's Memory Pool System (MPS, 1994+) is a flexible GC framework distinct from BDW's single-collector model: MPS supports both precise and conservative scanning and is structured around pool classes that the application chooses per allocation domain.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Ravenbrook's **Memory Pool System** (MPS, 1994+) is a flexible GC framework distinct from BDW's single-collector model: MPS supports **both precise and conservative scanning** and is structured around **pool classes** that the application chooses per allocation domain.

A program can mix mark-sweep (`AMC`), copying (`AMS`), automatic mark-compact, and reference-counted pools in one heap, with MPS providing the underlying root scanning, write barriers, and GC scheduling.

sections.title: Per-pool memory-management policy
sections.content:
The architectural lesson is **per-pool memory-management policy in one runtime**. The same program can allocate Java-style objects into a generational copying pool, COBOL-style flat records into a manually-managed pool, and DSL-specific types into a custom pool, all sharing one root set and one collector cycle.

This is similar in spirit to Verona's per-region pluggable strategies (region-typed languages enforce the choice in the type system) but MPS provides the same flexibility as a library for languages that do not have language-level region typing.

sections.title: Verification heritage
sections.content: The implementation has unusually rich verification heritage: MPS's hot-path code is **specified in a formal modelling language** (the "MPS protocol") and large parts of the codebase have been reviewed for memory-management invariants by professional verifiers. **Twenty-plus years of continuous maintenance with no major reported memory-safety bug** is an unusual record for a 60 KLOC C codebase performing concurrent root scanning.

sections.title: Production deployments
sections.content:
Production: Open Dylan's runtime is MPS-based; **Configura's CET designer** (a CAD tool) has shipped on MPS since the 1990s; the collector also underpins several proprietary financial-systems runtimes.

Status (as of 2026-04): commercial support and licensing via Ravenbrook; MPS itself is BSD-licensed.
